judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with substantively equivalent findings; Report 2 is marginally more precise on category (d) (explicit 'remove it' for legacy_billing_secret_arn) and on the migration/runbook contradiction, but the difference is minor.

report_1.evidence: Line 3, `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` — relaxed authentication in a multi-tenant medical scheduling product is the most severe item here; set to `"false"`... Line 4, `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` — a baked-in QA superadmin account in production; remove it (empty string)... Line 6, `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"` ... Set the sink to `""` (or a dedicated prod path if shadow scoring is intended). ... Line 7, `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key"` — production reuses the exact secret ARN staging uses; production should reference its own secret, not share credentials with staging.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:3` — `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`: relaxed authentication in a multi-tenant healthcare app is the most dangerous line here. Remove entirely. ... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:5–6` ... Remove both keys entirely. ... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:7` — `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key"`: a legacy-billing secret injection carried over from staging. Nothing in the new environment should recreate this fossil; remove it

report_1.evidence: With `--mode staging`, Vite loads `frontend/.env.staging`, which sets `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com`. The bundle is then baked into a Docker image (lines 19–21) and that **same image digest** is deployed to staging (line 28) and then to prod (line 34). Consequence: the production frontend will ship with the staging API URL hardcoded into its JavaScript. There is no `frontend/.env.production` file in the repo, so even changing `--mode` wouldn't work yet — the file must be created and either a separate prod build added or the URL made a runtime concern.

report_2.evidence: it is baked in, not read at runtime... There is no `frontend/.env.production` in the repo and no production-mode build anywhere, so the production deploy (`deploy.yml:34-35` — "deploy the same already-built digest to prod") would ship a bundle whose API base URL points at `https://staging-api.example.com`... Prod needs its own build with `--mode production` and a `.env.production`, and ideally a check that scans the built bundle for staging identifiers.

report_1.evidence: **Migrations**: `docs/prod-runbook.md` (last updated 3 months ago, pre-dating prod) claims migrations run automatically during deploy, but nothing visible in the workflow does this — presumably it's inside the missing `deploy.sh`, which can't be verified. The prod database would need migrations `0001`–`0003` applied on first launch.

report_2.evidence: **No migration step.** `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in `deploy.yml` runs the three Alembic migrations in `backend/migrations/versions/`. The runbook is self-declared "last updated: 3 months ago" and is contradicted by the actual pipeline — the production database would come up with no schema unless migrations are run manually.

report_1.evidence: For production, `infra/prod/main.tf:3` was copied unchanged and **still trusts `…:environment:staging`**. ... production deploys can't assume the prod role. ... The fix: parameterize the role name and set prod's `oidc_sub_condition` to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`.

report_2.evidence: Production (`infra/prod/main.tf:3`) was copied without editing and **also** trusts `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging`. ... Prod needs its own role with its own condition `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`, plus the role-name deduplication noted in answer 1.
